findings.detail: 5 agents (nel/sam/ra/aether/dex) flagged dead-loop on the 14:01Z cycle — pattern persists across 6+ consecutive probes. Auto-remediation dispatches are not breaking the loop because the 'stuck' state IS the agents' self-reported assessment ('routine maintenance', 'metrics cycle complete'), not actual halt. Structural fix needed: either (a) change dead-loop detector to ignore healthy steady-state, or (b) require agents emit a growth/ARIC ticket when assessment is unchanged 3+ cycles.

Last 2h: 40 DELEGATE / 237 REPORT / 0 RESOLVE in log.jsonl. Reports flow in but nothing reaches RESOLVE state. Same pattern as prior healthcheck's '16 P1 DELEGATED tasks still open' headline. Requires cascade receipt tracking or explicit RESOLVE action on REPORT match.

21 failed jobs in kai/queue/failed/ older than 24h, including 2 zero-byte sentinels (00e62347, 4ae87c70) and cmd-1776912672-157 blocked-by-security-check. Archive or triage at next interactive session.
hyo category still shows no output today (expected — hyo is not a runner-driven agent). Suppressable false positive from prior healthcheck.

Last big commit (f45be04 at 13:50Z, 51 files, 4.9k+ insertions) landed clean. kai/tickets/tickets.jsonl is now 51.89 MB — GitHub LFS warning emitted on push. Consider rolling ticket archive before file crosses 100 MB hard limit.

actions_taken: Logged findings. No new dispatches — prior 14:01Z healthcheck already dispatched auto-remediation for 5 P1 dead-loops; repeating that in the same window would compound the delegation-closure backlog.

recent_queue_results.command: cd ~/Documents/Projects/Hyo && git add -A && git commit -m 'Research + implement: autonomous company
